stat_of_week: 19 HR / 39 RBI - BaseballPro77 set new single-week league records in both home runs and RBI simultaneously, powered by Mike Trout's 6-HR, 11-RBI, .459 OBP monster week
thriller_note: The only matchup that ended in a tie, with both teams splitting categories razor-thin across the board — Canberra needed a Henderson bounceback and Riverside left saves on the table, leaving both teams hungry for a clean win heading into Week 5.
key_storyline: BaseballPro77 delivered the most dominant offensive week in league history, setting new records with 19 HR and 39 RBI while rolling to a 7-2 blowout over Wisconsin Rapids Reapers — Mike Trout alone outscored several full rosters with a 10-run, 6-HR, 11-RBI, .459 OBP performance that looked like a video game. Combined with General Hospital's league-best 8 category wins and a staff ERA of 2.68, the top of this league is pulling away from the field fast. The real question heading into Week 5 is whether anyone in the middle tier has the firepower to slow either of these juggernauts down.
lucky_reason: Benchwarmers posted a league-worst 4 HR, just 45 K, and finished bottom-five in most offensive categories, yet their luck delta of +1.21 is the highest in the league — Michael King carried them to categories they had no business winning.
unlucky_reason: Despite José Ramírez posting a .500 OBP with 4 HR and 5 SB, Yordan Alvarez going deep four times, and winning 6 categories, the Twins carry the league's worst luck delta at -0.61, meaning their gaudy numbers keep not translating into the wins their talent deserves.
records_broken: BaseballPro77 broke two league records simultaneously in Week 4: most HR in a single week (19, surpassing the previous mark) and most RBI in a single week (39, a new league high).
power_rankings: 1. BaseballPro77 | Set league records in HR and RBI, Trout is in full MVP mode, and the pitching held a 2.33 ERA — no one else is playing at this level right now | same
2. General Hospital | Led the league with 8 category wins, 77.1 IP, 6 QS, and a 2.68 ERA; added Kumar Rocker to an already deep rotation that is becoming truly frightening | same
3. Sugar Land Skeeters | Dropped a 5-4 decision but remain the most consistent team in the league across all four weeks; Tatis, Happ, and Weathers give them multi-category upside nightly | same
4. Athletic Park Dragons | Judge's 6-HR week was jaw-dropping and Sánchez/Ober both delivered; this team is rounding into a genuine contender with pitching depth to match the offense | same
5. Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | A league-leading 8 QS and 2.49 ERA remain the identity; Skenes' 0.90 ERA and smart waiver additions like Masyn Winn show an active, forward-thinking manager | same

6. Abbotsford-Colby Twins | Ramirez and Alvarez each went off for 4 HR and the team won 6 categories — the talent is unquestionable, but a -0.61 luck delta is a brutal ongoing tax on a top-five roster | up

7. North Correa | Chris Sale's 0.69 ERA anchored the week and the Ohtani-Hoerner combo gives them a high floor; Wells and Rocchio upgrades at catcher and middle infield show smart roster management | same
8. Hiroshima Radiation | McLean and Eovaldi combined for 5 QS and sub-2.00 ERAs again, but a -0.39 luck delta and an offense that managed just 6 HR and 1 SB are real ceiling concerns | down
9. Albuquerque Bombers | Buxton's 3-HR week sparked a 5-4 win and the roster is actively being patched with four waiver additions; the activity is encouraging but the holes are real | same
10. Riverside Roughnecks | Tied when a win looked probable; Langeliers, Kurtz, and Misiorowski are legitimate assets but the team still can't string enough categories together consistently | same
11. Canberra Wombats | A tie beats a loss but Henderson's .189 OBP is alarming for a cornerstone player; the pitching inventory is thin and the Week 5 draw against Wonder Wharf is a tough ask | same

12. Wisconsin Rapids Reapers | Got steamrolled 7-2 by the league's hottest team; Ben Rice is a real piece but 33 hits and a .257 OBP league-wide signal a roster that needs significant offensive upgrades | down

13. Buttville Skidmarks | Reynolds posted a .500 OBP again and it meant nothing; the pitching posted a 4.87 ERA and the team managed just 3 category wins — the same structural problems, the same result | same

14. Benchwarmers | Michael King's 19 K effort is elite and real, but a +1.21 luck delta masking 4 HR, 45 K, and bottom-five offense means regression is coming fast unless major additions are made | same
waiver_highlights: Wonder Wharf Wonderdogs | A | Added Paul Skenes-level depth with Masyn Winn (SS), Dustin May (SP), and Scott Barlow (RP) in one week | A fully loaded move that adds positional flexibility, rotation depth, and bullpen help simultaneously — this manager is building a dynasty

General Hospital | A- | Added Kumar Rocker (SP); dropped Leiter, Junk, and Javier | Cleaning house on underperforming arms and upgrading to a high-ceiling starter is exactly what a first-place team should do to protect its lead

Albuquerque Bombers | B+ | Added Keaton Winn, Braxton Garrett, Noelvi Marte, and Josh Jung; dropped four roster fillers | Four additions in one week signals urgency — Jung and Marte add lineup versatility and the SP depth addresses a real weakness

North Correa | B | Added Austin Wells (C) and Brayan Rocchio (2B,SS); dropped Realmuto and Brooks Lee | Upgrading catcher from an aging Realmuto to the breakout Wells is a savvy move; Rocchio adds middle infield depth at a position of need

Riverside Roughnecks | C+ | Added Victor Vodnik (RP); dropped Mark Vientos (1B,3B) | Losing Vientos' power upside for a reliever is a questionable trade-off for a team that already struggles to score runs
